name: Tenderly Web3 Development Platform API
what_it_does: Web3 development platform with REST and GraphQL APIs for simulating, debugging, and monitoring EVM smart contracts — providing transaction simulation, contract debugging, real-time alerting, and forks for testing without deploying to mainnet.
use_cases: Simulating transactions before on-chain execution to predict gas costs and reverts
Debugging smart contract reverts with human-readable stack traces via API
Setting up real-time alerts for smart contract events and anomalies
Creating virtual test networks (forks) for safe smart contract development
Monitoring DeFi protocol events and suspicious transactions programmatically
not_for: Non-EVM blockchains — Tenderly supports EVM-compatible chains only
Data analytics at scale without smart contract focus (use Dune or Flipside)
Production RPC node service without development features (use QuickNode)
best_when: A smart contract developer or agent needs to simulate, test, and monitor EVM contracts with detailed debugging and real-time monitoring without managing blockchain infrastructure.
avoid_when: You need raw blockchain data analytics without smart contract development focus.

auth.notes: API key passed as X-Access-Key header. Project-scoped endpoints. Account-level and project-level access. Webhook signing for alert delivery.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: limited simulations, 1 project, basic monitoring
pricing.notes: Credit-based for simulations and forks. Developer plan for serious smart contract development. Enterprise for production-grade monitoring at DeFi protocol scale.

performance.rate_limits: Simulation rate limits per plan; fork creation limited; alerts unlimited on paid

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Excellent REST API documentation with OpenAPI reference. Simulation endpoints return human-readable traces and decoded events. Error responses include revert reasons when simulating failed transactions. Webhook delivery for alert events.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Transaction simulations are idempotent (same inputs, same result). Fork creation idempotent by fork ID. Alert configuration updates are idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Fork networks are ephemeral — forks expire and must be refreshed periodically
Simulation results reflect chain state at block number — specify block for consistency
Alert webhook delivery may have delay — design for eventual delivery
Gas estimation from simulation may differ from actual on-chain execution

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 compliant. API key via header (more secure than query param). TLS enforced. Webhook signatures for secure alert delivery. Project-level isolation. EU data residency available.
